title: Released - Webhook to know when an item is deleted/archived
excerpt: Is it possible to have a webhook that notifies when an item has been deleted or archived?

posts.author: turboroof (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: Is it possible to have a webhook that notifies when an item has been deleted or archived?

posts.author: Scott-monday.com (Scott Underwood)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @turboroof

monday.com does not currently have any webhooks that are triggered upon item deletion or archival, but I’m happy to share this feedback with the team! I can certainly see how this would be useful.

Let us know if you have any other questions or suggestions!

-Scott

posts.author: turboroof (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any update on this? I really need it. I constantly have to update my external databases manually and it’s very time consuming.

posts.author: turboroof (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: hi Scott, is there any update on this feature? It would make my life so much easier since I need to sync up my database with some pulses from Monday

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @turboroof - Daniel stepping in for Scott here.

We don’t quite have an update on this feature at the time - but I can definitely see how this would be something useful to have. This request has been shared with the team internally and we hope to tackle it down the line, but I wouldn’t quite be able to provide an ETA right now.

posts.author: turboroof (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: thank you Daniel, I look forward to have this feature available

posts.author: grt-s (Sam)
posts.cookedHtml: I too would very much appreciate such a webhook.

posts.author: zlanich (Zach Lanich)
posts.cookedHtml: I too need this web hook. Is there any plan yet to implement this?

posts.author: willemdewulf (Will)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1, We too need a web hook to detect deleted/archived items.
Is there an ETA yet? or a workaround to know the recently deleted items in a board?

posts.author: mmoulton (Mike Moulton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday team, can you please provide an update on this.

It’s extremely difficult to manage our external integrations at the scale of our account usage without access to one of the fundamental pillars of the CRUD data lifecycle.

This becomes especially difficult when you also impose arbitrary API limits and extreme “costs” to execute queries that need to check for items that have been deleted/archived.

posts.author: Adurm (Adam Cranch)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi.

Im perplexed that this isnt an integrated webhook already. Please listen to the community and at least get the basics done.

posts.author: andhikatj (Andhika)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any update on this topic? This is fundamental. Also need it urgently as we’re currently manually updating our database with wrong data

@dsilva @monday-team

posts.author: mmoulton (Mike Moulton)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Helen would you be able to provide an update on this functionality?

In lieu of a formal delete hook, if the update item/group/board hook would fire when the ‘deleted’, ‘archived’, or ‘status’ fields change, this would likely be enough for most use cases I have.

This is really a critical piece of functionality that is missing.

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

I’m afraid I don’t have an update to provide at this time. I can definitely understand how important this feature is to have, so I’m going to bump our development team again.

For now, I would definitely recommend polling and querying your boards in a timely basis to see which of your items have been deleted.

Thank you for your patience!

posts.author: Jaap-CAD2M (Jaap Holweg)
posts.cookedHtml: An item_deleted event is indeed needed - periodically polling all items and checking them on another system (Google Calendar for many users in my case) is not feasible. The alternative would be to introduce an intermediate database for the sole purpose of detecting deletions - that’s crazy. So my vote for an item_deleted event (webhook or custom integration) is here!

posts.author: turboroof (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m already starting to migrate that type of automations out of monday since I’ve waited for a long time for this feature

posts.author: Adurm (Adam Cranch)
posts.cookedHtml: This seems a ridiculous suggestion considering that API calls are throttled.

posts.author: mingoes (mingoes)
posts.cookedHtml:
My best option for now is to change a status to “mark for deletion” and fire off a webhook that internally handles the deletion and as a final step removes it from the board.

Not ideal since I need to ensure the team uses this method… but it’s less intense than polling.

posts.author: Adurm (Adam Cranch)
posts.cookedHtml:
this is a solid suggestion for a temp workaround.

Still annoying, but i think its the best workaround suggested so far.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Adurm @Helen @turboroof !

There’s a way to create a webhook trigger that goes off when an item is removed.
You can use Delete an Item Webhook.

It’ll give you a recipe where you add a button and a URL for a webhook.

image1756×636 50.5 KB

If you leave the Webhook URL entry blank (just hit the space bar or enter a non valid url), the app will still delete the item, but the webhook wont go off. This way you can set the automation yourself with the button configuration.

Of course, you can do both at the same time.

Note for developers:
The webhook address will receive a post request with the same body and headers only this time the body’s payload will contain a new property called “deletedItem”.
It will contain everything about the deleted item (including the column values).

 payload: {
    blockKind: 'action',
    blockMetadata: null,
    inboundFieldValues: {
      boardId: 1577193513,
      itemId: 1577303761,
      columnId: 'button',
      delButtonId: 'button',
      webhook: 'https://your.webhook.address'
    },
    inputFields: {
      boardId: 1577303303,
      itemId: 1577303333,
      columnId: 'button',
      delButtonId: 'button',
      webhook: 'https://your.webhook.address'
    },
    recipeId: 30336633,
    forceNoCharge: false,
    integrationId: 56133926
  },
  deletedItem: {
    id: '1577303333',
    name: 'New Item',
    column_values: [ [Object], [Object], [Object], [Object] ],
    created_at: '2021-08-15T14:06:17Z',
    group: { id: 'topics', title: 'Group Title' },
    updates: []
  }

I really hope this helps.

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Yoggie,

That’s amazing! Thanks so much for sharing your solution with the community I appreciate you helping out other developers, while we are still not quite there yet with our platform’s features.

-Alex

posts.author: Dontan (Masaaki Goto)
posts.cookedHtml: I need this web hook too. Is there any plan yet to implement this?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Dontan!

Welcome to the community! I hope you like it here

As of today, I am not aware of this webhook being implemented in the near future. Having said that, I will add your vote to this request so hopefully, we can count on this soon.

Cheers,
-Matias

posts.author: hrickards (Harry Rickards)
posts.cookedHtml: For anyone discovering this post through Google, this has now been added to Monday: New additions to the webhooks family!
